audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Zelophehad-daughters tribal-inheritance conclusion, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Gileadite clan petition, concern over intertribal marriage and Jubilee, marriage-within-clan ruling, daughters' compliance, retained Manasseh inheritance, and final plains-of-Moab colophon. Strong gender-agency and anti-coercion safeguards required.

verses.restored: Heads of fathers' houses of clan of sons of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from clans of sons of Joseph, came near and spoke before Moses and before leaders, heads of fathers' houses of children of Israel.
They said, YHWH commanded my lord to give land as inheritance by lot to children of Israel, and my lord was commanded by YHWH to give inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters.

If they become wives to one from sons of other tribes of children of Israel, then their inheritance will be withdrawn from inheritance of our fathers and added to inheritance of tribe to which they become; so it will be withdrawn from lot of our inheritance.

When Jubilee comes for children of Israel, their inheritance will be added to inheritance of tribe to which they become, and from inheritance of tribe of our fathers their inheritance will be withdrawn.
Moses commanded children of Israel at mouth/command of YHWH, saying, Tribe of sons of Joseph speaks rightly.
This is word YHWH commanded concerning daughters of Zelophehad, saying: To whom is good in their eyes they may become wives, only into clan of tribe of their father they shall become wives.
No inheritance of children of Israel shall turn from tribe to tribe, for children of Israel shall cling each to inheritance of tribe of his fathers.
Every daughter possessing inheritance from tribes of children of Israel shall become wife to one from clan of tribe of her father, so children of Israel may possess each inheritance of his fathers.
No inheritance shall turn from one tribe to another tribe, for tribes of children of Israel shall cling each to its inheritance.
As YHWH commanded Moses, so daughters of Zelophehad did.
Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to sons of their father's brothers.
They became wives within clans of sons of Manasseh son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained with tribe of clan of their father.
These are commands and judgments that YHWH commanded by hand of Moses to children of Israel in plains of Moab by Jordan at Jericho.

translation_notes: The chapter revisits the daughters' victory in Numbers 27 because tribal leaders fear that marriage could transfer land between tribes.
The ruling preserves the daughters' right to choose—'to whom is good in their eyes'—but restricts the eligible group to their father's clan. Agency and patriarchal land control coexist in tension.
The land concern is tied to tribal inheritance and Jubilee, not a claim that women are incapable of holding property.
Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah act within the ruling and retain their inheritance inside Manasseh.
The book ends with women whose petition changed law, even though the final settlement still constrains their marriage choices.
This ancient tribal property rule must not be generalized into modern bans on interfaith, interracial, intercaste, queer, or freely chosen marriage.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 36 to control whom women marry, force endogamy, deny bodily autonomy, preserve family wealth through coercion, or prohibit interracial, interfaith, intercaste, or queer relationships. Honor the daughters' legal courage while naming the final rule's patriarchal constraint.
